name: Top Dollar Pawn & Gun LLC
description_short: Licensed pawnbroker in Gretna, LA offering collateral-based loans against firearms and other valuables. ATF-regulated FFL dealer with current federal licensing through June 2027.
description_long:
Top Dollar Pawn & Gun LLC is a federally licensed pawnbroker operating in Gretna, Louisiana, specializing in firearms-based collateral lending. The business holds an active Federal Firearms License (FFL #5-72-051-02-7F-05040) issued by the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives, with current licensing valid through June 1, 2027. As a Type 02 licensee, the company is specifically authorized to operate as a pawnbroker in firearms other than destructive devices.

Top Dollar Pawn & Gun operates as a traditional pawnbroker, providing short-term collateral-based loans to customers who pledge firearms, guns, or other personal property as security. The business maintains compliance with federal firearms regulations and operates under ATF oversight, which requires adherence to strict licensing, record-keeping, and transfer protocols outlined in 27 CFR 478.94. This regulatory framework ensures that all firearm-related transactions meet federal standards.

The company distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on firearms as collateral in a regulated lending environment. Unlike informal lending arrangements, Top Dollar Pawn & Gun's FFL status provides customers with transparency regarding federal compliance and legitimacy. The business has maintained its federal license through regular renewal cycles, indicating sustained operational compliance with ATF requirements.

As a pawnbroker, this business serves customers seeking quick liquidity against tangible assets without traditional credit requirements. However, customers should understand that pawn lending typically involves higher effective interest rates compared to conventional personal loans, and failure to reclaim collateral within agreed timeframes results in permanent loss of the pledged item. The company's firearms specialization means it is best suited for customers with guns or firearms-related items to pledge, rather than those seeking unsecured personal credit.
pros: Holds active Federal Firearms License with current authorization through June 2027
Licensed by ATF, providing regulatory oversight and legal compliance verification
Established business location in Gretna, LA with verifiable contact information (504-367-1122)
No credit check required; collateral-based lending available to all customers
Immediate liquidity available against firearms and personal property collateral
Federal licensing ensures compliance with 27 CFR 478.94 firearms transfer regulations
Registered on FFLs.com database for transparency and public record verification
cons: Pawn lending typically carries high effective interest rates on short-term loans
Customers risk losing pledged firearms or valuables if unable to repay within specified timeframe
Limited service scope—primarily focused on firearms rather than diverse collateral categories
No information provided on website regarding specific loan terms, APR, or repayment schedules
ATF-regulated lending means strict record-keeping requirements may limit flexibility
best_for: Gun owners or firearms collectors needing immediate short-term cash against their collection
Customers with poor credit or no credit history seeking collateral-based lending alternatives
Individuals with firearms inventory seeking to convert assets to liquidity quickly
diagnosis: Top Dollar Pawn & Gun LLC is best suited for firearm owners or collectors needing immediate short-term cash who are willing to pledge firearms or valuable items as collateral. The main caveat is that pawn loans carry substantially higher effective interest rates than traditional personal loans, and customers risk permanent loss of their pledged items if unable to repay within the agreed timeframe.
services: Collateral-based pawn loans secured by firearms
Federal Firearms License (FFL) compliance and firearms transfers
Short-term liquidity against personal property collateral
